title: Rule IR
description: A single design/manufacturing rule in intermediate representation. One deck is a list of these. Authored by humans or extracted (with review) from vendor rule sheets; consumed by the package-lint engine and the escape oracle.

id.description: Stable unique identifier for this rule within a deck (e.g. 'SUB.TRACE.WIDTH.MIN.M1').

source.description: Provenance anchor: where in the originating document this rule came from.

scope.description: Optional narrowing of where the rule applies (layer class, net class, region, component class). Absent fields mean 'applies everywhere'.

parameter.description: Controlled-vocabulary name of the quantity constrained. Known values map to implemented checks; unknown values are NOT schema errors — they surface as 'coverage: unimplemented' at run time (honest-coverage doctrine). Conventional vocabulary: trace_width_min, spacing_min, degas_to_trace_clearance_min, degas_coverage_window, copper_balance_window, annular_ring_min, ball_grid, copper_to_edge_min.

value.description: The constraint value: a scalar with units, a restricted-AST expression, or a piecewise table keyed by a design-variable condition.

tier.description: Capability tier the rule belongs to (conventionally 'normal' or 'advanced', but vendor-specific tier names are allowed). Absent means the rule applies to all tiers.

severity.description: Enforcement weight. 'hard' gates tape-out and drives nonzero CLI exit codes; 'preferred' is a strong recommendation; 'advisory' is informational.

conditions.description: Zero or more predicates that must hold for the rule to apply. A structured condition is machine-evaluable; a free-text condition cannot be evaluated and therefore forces executability to 'manual'.

executability.description: How the rule is (or is not) mechanically checkable, routing it to a class of check implementation. 'manual' means no automatic check exists and a human must verify.

routing.description: Which subsystem consumes the rule: 'cm' (constraint manager / connectivity), 'dfx' (design-for-x geometry), 'external' (the geometry lint engine), or 'checklist' (human checklist item).

lifecycle.description: Revision lifecycle: when the rule became effective and whether it is deprecated.

doc.description: Document name or identifier the rule was taken from.

page.description: Page number or label within the document.

row.description: Row identifier within the referenced table.

layer_class.description: Layer class the rule applies to (e.g. 'signal', 'plane', 'buildup').

net_class.description: Net class the rule applies to (e.g. 'high_speed', 'power').

region.description: Named physical region the rule is confined to.

component_class.description: Component class the rule applies to (e.g. 'bga', 'die').

kind.description: Discriminator: a single numeric value with units.
Discriminator: a value computed from a design variable via a restricted arithmetic expression.

expr.description: Restricted-AST arithmetic expression over one design variable (e.g. 'A * 0.10'). Must parse under the whitelist parser in pkgtk.schemas.expr — never eval'd.

variable.description: Name of the design variable the expression is evaluated over (default 'A').

kind.description: Discriminator: a table of (condition -> value) brackets over a design variable.

variable.description: Name of the design variable the bracket conditions are evaluated over (default 'A').

units.description: Units shared by every bracket value.

pieces.description: Ordered brackets. The first whose 'when' predicate is true supplies the value.

when.description: Restricted-AST comparison predicate over the design variable (e.g. '10 < A <= 14').

value.description: Value that applies when the predicate holds.

param.description: Name of the scoping attribute compared (e.g. 'layer_count', 'has_conductive_adhesive').

value.description: Right-hand comparison operand: a number, string, boolean, or array (for in / not_in).

text.description: Free-text condition that cannot be evaluated mechanically. Its presence forces the rule's executability to 'manual'.

effective_rev.description: Document revision at which this rule became effective.

deprecated.description: Whether the rule is deprecated and should no longer gate new designs.

deprecated_rev.description: Revision at which the rule was deprecated, if applicable.
